prompt: I'm thinking of using Redis for our job queue instead of a proper message broker like RabbitMQ. We have about 50 workers processing async tasks — image resizing, email sending, webhook delivery. Redis is simpler and we already run it for caching.

assertions.name: no-implementation
assertions.description: Response does not contain actual implementation code (small illustrative snippets OK)

assertions.name: identifies-3-plus-risks
assertions.description: Lists at least 3 distinct risks or failure modes of the proposed approach

assertions.name: proposes-alternative
assertions.description: Suggests at least 1 meaningfully different approach beyond Redis vs RabbitMQ binary

assertions.name: quantifies-tradeoffs
assertions.description: Includes at least 1 specific number, metric, or concrete data point

assertions.name: asks-probing-questions
assertions.description: Poses questions back to the user to deepen discussion

assertions.name: takes-a-position
assertions.description: States a clear opinion rather than just listing pros/cons neutrally

assertions.name: discussion-mode-signal
assertions.description: Makes clear this is an ongoing conversation, invites continued dialogue
